assertions.expectation: Explains that surge upgrades require extra compute capacity (maxSurge creates NEW nodes before draining old ones)
assertions.reason: Response explains that surge approach creates replacement nodes first and that the stuck upgrade is due to inability to create surge nodes due to quota constraints

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable >0 instead of relying on maxSurge (drains first, then creates — uses less quota)
assertions.reason: Response recommends drain-first approach with maxSurge=0 and maxUnavailable=1, explaining it drains nodes before creating replacements

assertions.expectation: Warns that maxUnavailable >0 means reduced capacity during upgrade — some pods won't have nodes
assertions.reason: Response explicitly warns about 'temporary capacity reduction (1 node at a time unavailable)' and 'temporary capacity loss during each node replacement'

assertions.expectation: Suggests temporarily scaling down non-critical deployments to free up quota for surge nodes
assertions.reason: Option 2 specifically suggests scaling down non-critical workloads with kubectl scale commands to free up quota

assertions.expectation: Mentions that reducing maxSurge to 1 minimizes extra quota needed (slower but fits within constraints)
assertions.reason: Response mentions using 'maxSurge=1' as a middle ground and explains it creates only 1 surge node at a time to fit within minimal extra quota

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to update surge settings (maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1 for zero-extra-capacity mode)
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact gcloud command with --max-surge-upgrade 0 and --max-unavailable-upgrade 1 for zero-extra-capacity mode

assertions.expectation: Recommends doing the upgrade in off-peak hours when fewer pods are running
assertions.reason: Option 3 suggests scheduling during nights/weekends when autoscaling has scaled down pods, and the recommended approach mentions scheduling during off-peak hours

assertions.expectation: Mentions requesting a temporary quota increase as a faster alternative to a permanent increase
assertions.reason: Option 4 mentions contacting Cloud Customer Care for emergency quota increase and explains same-day emergency quota increases are sometimes possible for urgent operational needs
